question:
In a heat exchanger, hot water enters at 64.0°C / 2.3 MPa (2.1 kg/s) and exits at 47.0°C. Liquid R-134a enters the cold side at 14.0°C / 1.48 MPa (9.6 kg/s). Both streams remain liquid. Determine the heat transfer rate and the R-134a exit temperature.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg
h_c_in = ___ kJ/kg
Q̇ = ___ kW
h_c_out = ___ kJ/kg
T_c_out = ___ °C
raw_response:
Since both streams remain liquid, the liquid/compressed-liquid approximation is appropriate, so enthalpy is taken mainly as a function of temperature: \(h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)\).

Hot water side:
\[
\dot Q = \dot m_h\,(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})
\]
Using liquid-water enthalpies at 64.0°C and 47.0°C:
\[
h_{h,in}\approx 267.9,\quad h_{h,out}\approx 196.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
\dot Q = 2.1(267.9-196.8)=149.3\ \text{kW}
\]

Cold R-134a side:
\[
\dot Q = \dot m_c\,(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})
\]
Using liquid R-134a enthalpy at 14.0°C:
\[
h_{c,in}\approx 72.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
h_{c,out}=72.0+\frac{149.3}{9.6}=87.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
From liquid R-134a properties, \(h_{c,out}\approx 87.5\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to about \(25.0^\circ\text{C}\) at 1.48 MPa, so it remains liquid.

h_c_in = 72.0 kJ/kg

h_c_out = 87.5 kJ/kg

question:
Hot water at 60.0°C / 0.9 MPa (4.6 kg/s) heats liquid R-134a entering at 12.0°C / 1.45 MPa (9.9 kg/s). The hot water exits at 49.0°C. Both streams remain liquid. Find the heat transfer rate, R-134a exit temperature, and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg
h_c_in = ___ kJ/kg
Q̇ = ___ kW
h_c_out = ___ kJ/kg
T_c_out = ___ °C
s_h_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_h_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
Treat both streams as compressed liquids, so use the usual approximation:

- water: \(h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)\), \(s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)\)
- liquid R-134a: \(h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)\), \(s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)\)

For the hot-water side,
\[
\dot Q=\dot m_h\,(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})
\]
Using liquid-water properties at 60°C and 49°C:
\[
h_{h,in}\approx 251.13,\quad h_{h,out}\approx 205.14\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
So
\[
\dot Q=4.6(251.13-205.14)\approx 211.8\ \text{kW}
\]

For the R-134a side,
\[
h_{c,out}=h_{c,in}+\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}
\]
Using compressed-liquid \(\approx\) saturated-liquid at 12°C:
\[
h_{c,in}\approx 67.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
Thus
\[
h_{c,out}\approx 67.9+\frac{211.8}{9.9}=89.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Using \(c_{p,\ell}\) for liquid R-134a \(\approx 1.42\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\),
\[
T_{c,out}\approx 12.0+\frac{211.8}{9.9(1.42)}\approx 27.1^\circ\text{C}
\]

Entropy generation for the whole heat exchanger (adiabatic to surroundings):
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_h(s_{h,out}-s_{h,in})+\dot m_c(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})
\]
Using liquid-water entropies:
\[
s_{h,in}\approx 0.8313,\quad s_{h,out}\approx 0.6906\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
For liquid R-134a at 12°C and 27.1°C:
\[
s_{c,in}\approx 0.271,\quad s_{c,out}\approx 0.344\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
Then
\[
\dot S_{gen}\approx 0.08\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_c_in = 67.9 kJ/kg

T_c_out = 27.1 °C

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg

s_c_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
Using an adiabatic heat exchanger with negligible kinetic/potential energy changes, and since both streams remain liquid, I used the compressed-liquid approximation:

- \( h(T,p) \approx h_f(T) \)
- \( s(T,p) \approx s_f(T) \)

Energy balance:
\[
\dot Q = \dot m_h\,(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})
\]
\[
h_{c,out}=h_{c,in}+\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}
\]

Entropy generation rate:
\[
\dot S_{gen}
=
\dot m_h\,(s_{h,out}-s_{h,in})
+
\dot m_c\,(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})
\]

Using liquid-water properties at 76°C and 37°C, and liquid R-134a properties at 11°C and the computed outlet state:

\[
\dot Q = 1.2\,(318.14-154.88)=195.91\ \text{kW}
\]
\[
h_{c,out}=67.32+\frac{195.91}{9.4}=88.16\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

From liquid R-134a properties, \(h_{c,out}\approx 88.16\ \text{kJ/kg}\) gives:
\[
T_{c,out}\approx 25.1^\circ\text{C}
\]

Then:
\[
\dot S_{gen}
=
1.2(0.5321-1.0283)+9.4(0.3316-0.2604)
=0.0738\ \text{kW/K}
\]

question:
Hot water at 82.0°C / 1.6 MPa (1.8 kg/s) heats liquid R-134a at 13.0°C / 1.45 MPa (8.0 kg/s) in a heat exchanger. The hot water exits at 39.0°C. Both streams remain liquid. T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine Q̇, T_c_out, Ṡ_gen, Ẋ_dest, and η_II.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_h_out = ___ kJ/kg
h_c_in = ___ kJ/kg
Q̇ = ___ kW
h_c_out = ___ kJ/kg
T_c_out = ___ °C
s_h_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_h_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_c_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using a steady, adiabatic heat-exchanger model:

- Energy balance:
  \[
  \dot Q=\dot m_h\,(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})=\dot m_c\,(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})
  \]
- For the liquid streams, use compressed-liquid/incompressible approximations:
  - water: \(h \approx c_p T,\; s \approx c_p \ln(T/T_{ref})\), \(c_{p,w}\approx 4.18\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
  - liquid R-134a: \(c_{p,c}\approx 1.42\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\), with the common refrigeration-table reference for \(h,s\)

Hot-water side:
\[
\dot Q = 1.8(4.18)(82-39)=323.53\ \text{kW}
\]

Cold side:
\[
\Delta h_c=\frac{\dot Q}{\dot m_c}=\frac{323.53}{8}=40.44\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
which gives \(T_{c,out}\approx 41.5^\circ\text{C}\).

Entropy generation for an adiabatic exchanger:
\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_h(s_{h,out}-s_{h,in})+\dot m_c(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})
\]

Exergy destruction:
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}
\]

Second-law efficiency:
\[
\eta_{II}=
\frac{\dot m_c\left[(h_{c,out}-h_{c,in})-T_0(s_{c,out}-s_{c,in})\right]}
{\dot m_h\left[(h_{h,in}-h_{h,out})-T_0(s_{h,in}-s_{h,out})\right]}
\]

h_h_out = 163.02 kJ/kg

s_c_out = 0.4261 kJ/(kg·K)

Ẋ_dest = 33.04 kW  
η_II = 0.0400

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 46.0°C and 5.4 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 313.0°C (constant pressure). Determine the heat input per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For a boiler operating at steady state with negligible changes in kinetic and potential energy and no shaft work,

\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]

### 1) Inlet enthalpy, compressed liquid
For compressed liquid water, use

\[
h_{in}\approx h_f(T)+v_f(T)\,[P-P_{sat}(T)]
\]

At \(T=46.0^\circ\text{C}\):

- \(h_f \approx 192.5\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(v_f \approx 0.00101\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)
- \(P_{sat} \approx 9.9\ \text{kPa}\)

So,

\[
h_{in}\approx 192.5 + (0.00101)(5400-9.9)
\]

\[
h_{in}\approx 197.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

### 2) Exit enthalpy, superheated steam
At constant pressure \(P=5.4\ \text{MPa}\) and \(T=313.0^\circ\text{C}\), from the superheated steam tables,

\[
h_{out}\approx 2976.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

### 3) Heat input per unit mass
\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}=2976.0-197.9=2778.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

h_in = 197.9 kJ/kg  
h_out = 2976.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 2778.1 kJ/kg

question:
A boiler at 3.0 MPa receives water at 42.0°C and produces steam at 474.0°C. The heat source temperature is 1555.0 K. Find q_in and s_gen per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Assume a steady-flow boiler with negligible shaft work and negligible changes in kinetic and potential energy.

Energy balance per unit mass:
\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]

Entropy balance per unit mass:
\[
s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]

Using standard water-property approximations/tables:

- Inlet water at \(42.0^\circ\text{C}\) is a compressed liquid, so use the compressed-liquid approximation:
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(42^\circ\text{C}),\quad s_{in}\approx s_f(42^\circ\text{C})
  \]
  giving
  \[
  h_{in}\approx 175.8\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_{in}\approx 0.5989\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

- Outlet steam at \(P=3.0\ \text{MPa},\ T=474.0^\circ\text{C}\) is superheated. From the superheated-steam table (interpolated),
  \[
  h_{out}\approx 3421.3\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_{out}\approx 7.1660\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

Then
\[
q_{in}=3421.3-175.8=3245.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

With \(T_{source}=1555.0\ \text{K}\),
\[
s_{gen}=7.1660-0.5989-\frac{3245.5}{1555.0}
=4.4798\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_in = 175.8 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3421.3 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3245.5 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.5989 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 7.1660 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 4.4798 kJ/(kg·K)

Assume a steady-flow boiler with negligible shaft work and negligible changes in kinetic and potential energy.

Energy balance per unit mass:
\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]

Entropy balance per unit mass:
\[
s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]

Using standard water-property approximations/tables:

- Inlet water at \(42.0^\circ\text{C}\) is a compressed liquid, so use the compressed-liquid approximation:
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(42^\circ\text{C}),\quad s_{in}\approx s_f(42^\circ\text{C})
  \]
  giving
  \[
  h_{in}\approx 175.8\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_{in}\approx 0.5989\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

- Outlet steam at \(P=3.0\ \text{MPa},\ T=474.0^\circ\text{C}\) is superheated. From the superheated-steam table (interpolated),
  \[
  h_{out}\approx 3421.3\ \text{kJ/kg},\quad s_{out}\approx 7.1660\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

Then
\[
q_{in}=3421.3-175.8=3245.5\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

With \(T_{source}=1555.0\ \text{K}\),
\[
s_{gen}=7.1660-0.5989-\frac{3245.5}{1555.0}
=4.4798\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_in = 175.8 kJ/kg

q_in = 3245.5 kJ/kg

question:
Water enters a boiler at 39.0°C and 13.0 MPa and exits as superheated steam at 559.0°C. The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1625.0 K. Determine the heat input and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Assume a steady-flow boiler with negligible changes in kinetic and potential energy, and negligible pressure drop so the water/steam remains at 13.0 MPa.

Using steam-table properties:

- **Inlet** (compressed liquid, 39.0°C, 13.0 MPa)  
  Approximate as compressed liquid using saturated-liquid data at 39.0°C:
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(39^\circ\mathrm{C})+v_f(39^\circ\mathrm{C})\,[P-P_{sat}]
  \]
  \[
  s_{in}\approx s_f(39^\circ\mathrm{C})
  \]
  This gives:
  \[
  h_{in}\approx 176.4\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_{in}\approx 0.558\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

- **Exit** (superheated steam, 13.0 MPa, 559.0°C)  
  From superheated steam tables:
  \[
  h_{out}\approx 3494.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_{out}\approx 6.640\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

### 1) Heat input per unit mass
For the boiler:
\[
q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}
\]
\[
q_{in}=3494.0-176.4=3317.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

### 2) Entropy generation per unit mass
Entropy balance for the boiler:
\[
s_{out}-s_{in}=\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}+s_{gen}
\]
So:
\[
s_{gen}=(s_{out}-s_{in})-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]
with \(T_{source}=1625.0\ \text{K}\):
\[
s_{gen}=(6.640-0.558)-\frac{3317.6}{1625.0}
\]
\[
s_{gen}=6.082-2.0416=4.040\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

h_in = 176.4 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3494.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3317.6 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.558 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.640 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 4.040 kJ/(kg·K)

\]
\[
s_{in}\approx s_f(74^\circ\text{C})
\]

For the exit state at \(6.2\ \text{MPa}\) and \(437.0^\circ\text{C}\), from superheated steam tables:

\[
h_{out}\approx 3255.9\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_{out}\approx 6.668\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

\]

Entropy balance for the boiler per unit mass:

\[
s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_{source}}
\]

with \(T_{source}=1879.0\ \text{K}\):

\[
s_{gen}=6.668-1.003-\frac{2939.8}{1879.0}

\]

question:
Water enters a boiler at 64.0°C and 10.3 MPa and exits as superheated steam at 518.0°C. The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 941.0 K. Determine the heat input and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Assume the boiler operates at essentially constant pressure, so

- Inlet state: compressed liquid water at \(T_1=64.0^\circ\text{C},\ P=10.3\ \text{MPa}\)
- Exit state: superheated steam at \(P=10.3\ \text{MPa},\ T_2=518.0^\circ\text{C}\)

Using standard water tables:

1. **Inlet (compressed liquid)**
   \[
   h_1 \approx h_f(64^\circ\text{C}) + v_f(64^\circ\text{C})\,[P-P_{sat}(64^\circ\text{C})]
   \]
   with \(h_f(64^\circ\text{C})\approx 267.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\), \(v_f\approx 0.00102\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\), giving
   \[
   h_1 \approx 278.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]
   For entropy of a compressed liquid, use
   \[
   s_1 \approx s_f(64^\circ\text{C}) \approx 0.880\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

2. **Outlet (superheated steam)**
   From superheated steam tables at \(10.3\ \text{MPa},\ 518^\circ\text{C}\),
   \[
   h_2 \approx 3417.0\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_2 \approx 6.650\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

3. **Heat input per unit mass**
   Steady-flow energy balance with negligible KE/PE changes and no shaft work:
   \[
   q_{in}=h_2-h_1
   \]
   \[
   q_{in}=3417.0-278.3=3138.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Entropy generation**
   Steady-flow entropy balance:
   \[
   s_2-s_1=\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}+s_{gen}
   \]
   where the heat source temperature is \(T_H=941.0\ \text{K}\).

   So
   \[
   s_{gen}=s_2-s_1-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
   \]
   \[
   s_{gen}=6.650-0.880-\frac{3138.7}{941.0}
   \]
   \[
   s_{gen}=2.434\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 76.0°C and 7.7 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 488.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1220.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using a steady-flow boiler model with negligible kinetic/potential energy changes and no shaft work:

- First law:  
  \( q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in} \)

- Inlet compressed liquid approximation:  
  \( h_{in} \approx h_f(76^\circ C) + v_f(76^\circ C)\,[P - P_{sat}(76^\circ C)] \)  
  \( s_{in} \approx s_f(76^\circ C) \)

- Outlet state from superheated steam tables at \(P=7.7\) MPa, \(T=488^\circ C\)

- Entropy balance per unit mass for heat transfer from a source at average temperature \(T_H=1220\) K:  
  \( s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \dfrac{q_{in}}{T_H} \)

- Exergy destruction:  
  \( x_{dest} = T_0\, s_{gen} \)

- Second-law efficiency of the boiler:  
  \( \eta_{II} = \dfrac{(h_{out}-h_{in}) - T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}{q_{in}\left(1-\dfrac{T_0}{T_H}\right)} \)

Using standard steam-table values/interpolation gives the following results (small variations are possible depending on the table set used):

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 52.0°C and 2.7 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 493.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1850.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady-flow energy, entropy, and exergy balances for the boiler:

1. **Inlet state (compressed liquid, 52.0°C, 2.7 MPa)**  
   Approximate compressed-liquid properties by saturated liquid at the same temperature:
   - \(h_f(52^\circ\text{C}) \approx 217.6\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
   - \(s_f(52^\circ\text{C}) \approx 0.730\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
   - \(v_f(52^\circ\text{C}) \approx 0.00101\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg}\)

   Enthalpy correction:
   \[
   h_{in} \approx h_f + v_f(P-P_{sat})
   \approx 217.6 + 0.00101(2700-13.5)
   \approx 220.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]
   \[
   s_{in} \approx s_f \approx 0.730\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
   \]

2. **Exit state (2.7 MPa, 493.0°C, superheated steam)**  
   From superheated steam tables:
   \[
   h_{out} \approx 3447.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad
   s_{out} \approx 7.286\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
   \]

3. **Heat input**
   \[
   q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in}
   = 3447.0 - 220.3
   = 3226.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Entropy generation**
   For heat transfer from combustion gases at average temperature \(T_H=1850.0\ \text{K}\):
   \[
   s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
   \]
   \[
   s_{gen} = (7.286-0.730) - \frac{3226.7}{1850.0}
   = 4.812\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
   \]

5. **Exergy destruction**
   \[
   x_{dest} = T_0 s_{gen} = 298.15(4.812) = 1434.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

6. **Second-law efficiency**
   Exergy increase of the water:
   \[
   \Delta \psi = (h_{out}-h_{in}) - T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})
   \]
   \[
   \Delta \psi = 3226.7 - 298.15(6.556) = 1271.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

   Exergy transfer with heat:
   \[
   x_Q = \left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right) q_{in}
   = \left(1-\frac{298.15}{1850.0}\right)(3226.7)
   = 2706.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

   \[
   \eta_{II} = \frac{\Delta \psi}{x_Q}
   = \frac{1271.9}{2706.7}
   = 0.470
   \]

h_in = 220.3 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3447.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3226.7 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.730 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 7.286 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 4.812 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 1434.8 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.470

   \[
   h_{out} \approx 3447.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad
   s_{out} \approx 7.286\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
   \]

3. **Heat input**
   \[
   q_{in} = h_{out} - h_{in}
   = 3447.0 - 220.3
   = 3226.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Entropy generation**
   For heat transfer from combustion gases at average temperature \(T_H=1850.0\ \text{K}\):
   \[
   s_{gen} = (s_{out}-s_{in}) - \frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
   \]
   \[
   s_{gen} = (7.286-0.730) - \frac{3226.7}{1850.0}
   = 4.812\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
   \]

5. **Exergy destruction**
   \[
   x_{dest} = T_0 s_{gen} = 298.15(4.812) = 1434.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

6. **Second-law efficiency**
   Exergy increase of the water:
   \[
   \Delta \psi = (h_{out}-h_{in}) - T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})
   \]
   \[
   \Delta \psi = 3226.7 - 298.15(6.556) = 1271.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

   Exergy transfer with heat:
   \[
   x_Q = \left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right) q_{in}
   = \left(1-\frac{298.15}{1850.0}\right)(3226.7)
   = 2706.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

   \[
   \eta_{II} = \frac{\Delta \psi}{x_Q}
   = \frac{1271.9}{2706.7}
   = 0.470
   \]

h_in = 220.3 kJ/kg  
h_out = 3447.0 kJ/kg  
q_in = 3226.7 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.730 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 7.286 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 4.812 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 1434.8 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.470

question:
Water enters a boiler as compressed liquid at 50.0°C and 4.0 MPa. It exits as superheated steam at 304.0°C (constant pressure). The heat is supplied by combustion gases at an average temperature of 1013.0 K. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C (298.15 K), P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the heat input, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_in = ___ kJ/kg
h_out = ___ kJ/kg
q_in = ___ kJ/kg
s_in = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_out = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady-flow boiler balances, neglecting KE/PE changes and shaft work:

- Inlet compressed liquid at 50.0°C, 4.0 MPa:
  \[
  h_{in}\approx h_f(50^\circ C)+v_f(50^\circ C)\,[P- P_{sat}(50^\circ C)]
  \]
  With \(h_f=209.33\ \text{kJ/kg},\ v_f=0.001012\ \text{m}^3/\text{kg},\ P_{sat}=12.35\ \text{kPa}\):
  \[
  h_{in}\approx 209.33+0.001012(4000-12.35)=213.37\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]
  \[
  s_{in}\approx s_f(50^\circ C)=0.7038\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

- Exit superheated steam at 4.0 MPa, 304.0°C:
  from superheated steam tables,
  \[
  h_{out}\approx 2971.2\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad
  s_{out}\approx 6.3815\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

- Energy balance:
  \[
  q_{in}=h_{out}-h_{in}=2971.2-213.37=2757.83\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

- Entropy balance with heat supplied from a source at \(T_H=1013.0\ \text{K}\):
  \[
  s_{gen}=s_{out}-s_{in}-\frac{q_{in}}{T_H}
  \]
  \[
  s_{gen}=6.3815-0.7038-\frac{2757.83}{1013.0}=2.955\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  x_{dest}=T_0 s_{gen}=298.15(2.955)=881.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
  \]

- Second-law efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_{II}=
  \frac{(h_{out}-h_{in})-T_0(s_{out}-s_{in})}
       {\left(1-\frac{T_0}{T_H}\right)q_{in}}
  \]
  \[
  \eta_{II}=
  \frac{2757.83-298.15(6.3815-0.7038)}
       {\left(1-\frac{298.15}{1013.0}\right)(2757.83)}
  =0.547
  \]

h_in = 213.37 kJ/kg  
h_out = 2971.20 kJ/kg  
q_in = 2757.83 kJ/kg  
s_in = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_out = 6.3815 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 2.955 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 881.6 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.547

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa. Stream 1: 179.0°C, 1.1 kg/s. Stream 2: 48.0°C, 7.9 kg/s. Find the exit temperature.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
raw_response:
At 1.2 MPa, the saturation temperature of water is about 188°C, so both inlet streams are **compressed liquids**. For compressed liquid water, a standard approximation is:

\[
h(T,P) \approx h_f(T)
\]

So we take the liquid enthalpy from the saturated-water temperature table at the given temperatures.

Mass balance:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.1+7.9=9.0\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Adiabatic mixing energy balance:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using saturated-liquid enthalpies:

- At \(179.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 758.9\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- At \(48.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 200.9\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Then:

\[
h_3=\frac{(1.1)(758.9)+(7.9)(200.9)}{9.0}=269.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now find the liquid temperature corresponding to \(h_3=269.1\ \text{kJ/kg}\). From the saturated-liquid table, this corresponds to about:

\[
T_3 \approx 64.3^\circ\text{C}
\]

h_1 = 758.9 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 200.9 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 9.0 kg/s  
h_3 = 269.1 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 64.3 °C

At 1.8 MPa, the saturation temperature is about 212°C, so both inlet streams are **compressed liquid water**. For compressed liquid water, a standard approximation is:

\]

- At \(T_1=171.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 723.6\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

\]

\]

From compressed-liquid/saturated-liquid water data, \(h_3 \approx 292.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to:

\[

In a mixing chamber, stream 1 (water at 181.0°C, 1.4 MPa, 8.5 kg/s) mixes with stream 2 (water at 50.0°C, 1.4 MPa, 8.9 kg/s). The mixing is adiabatic at constant pressure. Determine the exit temperature and mass flow rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
raw_response:
At 1.4 MPa, the saturation temperature of water is about 195 °C, so both inlet streams are **compressed liquid**.  
For compressed liquid water, use the common approximation:

\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)
\]

So from saturated-liquid water properties:

- At \(T_1=181.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx h_f(181^\circ\text{C}) \approx 767.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- At \(T_2=50.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx h_f(50^\circ\text{C}) = 209.3\ \text{kJ/kg}\)

Mass balance for steady mixing:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=8.5+8.9=17.4\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance for adiabatic mixing:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(8.5)(767.0)+(8.9)(209.3)}{17.4}=481.7\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now find the liquid-water temperature corresponding to \(h_3 \approx 481.7\ \text{kJ/kg}\), giving:

\[
T_3 \approx 114.8^\circ\text{C}
\]

ṁ_3 = 17.4 kg/s

  \]

  \dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
  \]
- Entropy balance for an adiabatic mixer:  
  \[
  \dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
  \]

- State 2: \(T_2=45.0^\circ\text{C}\) → compressed liquid, approximated by saturated liquid at \(45^\circ\text{C}\)

From steam tables and the balances, the outlet enthalpy falls between \(h_f\) and \(h_g\) at 0.9 MPa, so the exit is a saturated mixture and thus
\[
T_3=T_{\text{sat}}(0.9\text{ MPa})\approx 175.4^\circ\text{C}

\[

\]

- At \(T_2=54.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 226.1\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2 \approx 0.755\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}\)

\]

and

question:
Water at 146.0°C (3.0 kg/s) mixes adiabatically with water at 37.0°C (3.0 kg/s) at a constant pressure of 1.5 MPa. Find the exit temperature and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
Using the common compressed-liquid approximation for water,
\(h(T,p)\approx h_f(T)\) and \(s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)\).

Steady, adiabatic mixing with negligible KE/PE gives:

- Mass balance: \(\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=3+3=6\)
- Energy balance:  
  \[
  \dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
  \]
  Since the inlet flow rates are equal,
  \[
  h_3=\frac{h_1+h_2}{2}
  \]
- Entropy balance for an adiabatic mixer:
  \[
  \dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
  \]

From liquid-water properties at the given temperatures:

- At \(146.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1\approx 614.7\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1\approx 1.801\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- At \(37.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2\approx 154.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2\approx 0.532\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then:

\[
h_3=\frac{614.7+154.9}{2}=384.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

This corresponds to liquid water at about \(T_3\approx 91.8^\circ\text{C}\), with

\[
s_3\approx 1.214\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Finally,

\[
\dot S_{gen}=6(1.214)-3(1.801)-3(0.532)\approx 0.285\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_1 = 614.7 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 154.9 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.0 kg/s  
h_3 = 384.8 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 91.8 °C  
s_1 = 1.801 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.532 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.214 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.285 kW/K

question:
Water at 155.0°C (6.3 kg/s) mixes adiabatically with water at 36.0°C (5.0 kg/s) at a constant pressure of 2.0 MPa. Find the exit temperature and entropy generation rate.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
raw_response:
At \(P=2.0\) MPa, the saturation temperature of water is about \(212^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the exit stream are **compressed liquids**. For compressed liquid water, use the common approximation

\[
h(T,p)\approx h_f(T), \qquad s(T,p)\approx s_f(T)
\]

from saturated-liquid tables at the same temperature.

Steady, adiabatic mixing gives

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=6.3+5.0=11.3\ \text{kg/s}
\]

Energy balance:

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using saturated-liquid properties:

- at \(155.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_1 \approx 653.3\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_1 \approx 1.893\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)
- at \(36.0^\circ\text{C}\): \(h_2 \approx 150.8\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_2 \approx 0.519\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Then

\[
h_3=\frac{(6.3)(653.3)+(5.0)(150.8)}{11.3}\approx 431.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

From the saturated-liquid table, \(h_3\approx 431.0\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to

\[
T_3 \approx 102.8^\circ\text{C}, \qquad s_3 \approx 1.338\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Entropy rate balance for an adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=(11.3)(1.338)-(6.3)(1.893)-(5.0)(0.519)\approx 0.600\ \text{kW/K}
\]

h_2 = 150.8 kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = 11.3 kg/s

s_1 = 1.893 kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = 0.519 kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = 1.338 kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = 0.600 kW/K

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 160.0°C, 1.8 kg/s. Stream 2: 76.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady, adiabatic mixing at \(P=0.2\ \text{MPa}\):

- Mass balance:
  \[
  \dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=1.8+4.9=6.7\ \text{kg/s}
  \]

- Properties:
  - Stream 1 at \(0.2\ \text{MPa}, 160^\circ\text{C}\) is superheated vapor.
  - Stream 2 at \(76^\circ\text{C}\) is a compressed liquid; use the compressed-liquid approximation:
    \[
    h_2 \approx h_f(76^\circ\text{C}),\quad s_2 \approx s_f(76^\circ\text{C})
    \]

- Energy balance:
  \[
  \dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
  \]
  \[
  h_3=\frac{(1.8)h_1+(4.9)h_2}{6.7}
  \]

At \(0.2\ \text{MPa}\), the computed \(h_3\) lies between \(h_f\) and \(h_g\), so the exit is a saturated mixture, and therefore:
\[
T_3=T_{\text{sat}}(0.2\ \text{MPa})\approx 120.2^\circ\text{C}
\]

- Entropy balance for an adiabatic steady mixer:
  \[
  \dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
  \]

- Exergy destruction:
  \[
  \dot X_{\text{dest}}=T_0\dot S_{\text{gen}}
  \]
  with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\).

- Second-law efficiency, using
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{\dot X_{\text{out}}}{\dot X_{\text{in}}}
  =1-\frac{\dot X_{\text{dest}}}{\dot X_{\text{in}}}
  \]
  and
  \[
  \psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]
  relative to the dead state \((T_0=25^\circ\text{C},\ P_0=0.1\ \text{MPa})\).

Final results:

h_1 = 2783.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 318.1 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.7 kg/s  
h_3 = 980.3 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 120.2 °C  
s_1 = 7.310 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 1.024 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 2.739 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.177 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 52.8 kW  
η_II = 0.955

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.5 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 102.0°C, 2.4 kg/s. Stream 2: 58.0°C, 4.0 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
At \(P=0.5\) MPa, the saturation temperature of water is about \(151.8^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the outlet are **compressed liquid**.  
For compressed liquid water, a good approximation is

\[
h \approx c_p T, \qquad s \approx c_p \ln\!\left(\frac{T_K}{273.15}\right),
\]

with \(c_p \approx 4.18\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\).

### 1) Mass and energy balance
Steady, adiabatic mixing chamber:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=2.4+4.0=6.4\ \text{kg/s}
\]

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

Using \(h \approx c_p T(^\circ C)\):

\[
h_1 \approx 4.18(102.0)=426.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
h_2 \approx 4.18(58.0)=242.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(2.4)(426.4)+(4.0)(242.4)}{6.4}=311.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
T_3=\frac{h_3}{c_p}=74.5^\circ\text{C}
\]

### 2) Entropy balance
For adiabatic steady mixing:

\[
\dot S_{gen}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

Convert temperatures to K:

\[
T_1=375.15\ \text{K},\quad T_2=331.15\ \text{K},\quad T_3=347.65\ \text{K}
\]

\[
s_1=4.18\ln\!\left(\frac{375.15}{273.15}\right)=1.3263\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
\[
s_2=4.18\ln\!\left(\frac{331.15}{273.15}\right)=0.8049\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
\[
s_3=4.18\ln\!\left(\frac{347.65}{273.15}\right)=1.0081\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Thus,

\[
\dot S_{gen}=6.4(1.0081)-2.4(1.3263)-4.0(0.8049)=0.0494\ \text{kW/K}
\]

### 3) Exergy destruction
\[
\dot X_{dest}=T_0\dot S_{gen}
\]
with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\):

\[
\dot X_{dest}=298.15(0.0494)=14.7\ \text{kW}
\]

### 4) Second-law efficiency
Using the standard mixer exergetic efficiency

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot E_{x,out}}{\dot E_{x,in}}
\]

For liquid water at the same pressure, the flow exergy can be approximated by

\[
\psi \approx c_p\left[(T-T_0)-T_0\ln\!\left(\frac{T}{T_0}\right)\right]+v(P-P_0)
\]

This gives approximately

\[
\dot E_{x,in}\approx 116.4\ \text{kW},\qquad
\dot E_{x,out}\approx 101.7\ \text{kW}
\]

so

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{101.7}{116.4}\approx 0.874
\]

h_1 = 426.4 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 242.4 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 6.4 kg/s  
h_3 = 311.4 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 74.5 °C  
s_1 = 1.3263 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.8049 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.0081 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.0494 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 14.7 kW  
η_II = 0.874

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 1.2 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 237.0°C, 4.9 kg/s. Stream 2: 33.0°C, 4.2 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using steady, adiabatic mixing with negligible KE/PE changes:

- Mass balance:  
  \[
  \dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2
  \]
- Energy balance:  
  \[
  \dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
  \]
- Entropy balance for an adiabatic mixer:  
  \[
  \dot S_{\mathrm{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
  \]
- Exergy destruction:  
  \[
  \dot X_{\mathrm{dest}}=T_0\dot S_{\mathrm{gen}}
  \]
- Second-law efficiency (exergy of product / exergy of fuel):  
  \[
  \eta_{II}=\frac{\dot m_3\psi_3}{\dot m_1\psi_1+\dot m_2\psi_2},
  \qquad
  \psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
  \]

Using steam-table properties at \(P=1.2\ \text{MPa}\):

- State 1 (\(237^\circ\text{C}\), superheated): \(h_1 \approx 2897.0\), \(s_1 \approx 6.754\)
- State 2 (\(33^\circ\text{C}\), compressed liquid, approximated by saturated liquid at same \(T\)): \(h_2 \approx 139.3\), \(s_2 \approx 0.477\)

Then:

\[
\dot m_3 = 4.9+4.2=9.1\ \text{kg/s}
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(4.9)(2897.0)+(4.2)(139.3)}{9.1}\approx 1624.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

At \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), this enthalpy is in the two-phase region, so the exit is a saturated mixture and:

\[
T_3=T_{\text{sat}}(1.2\ \text{MPa})\approx 188.2^\circ\text{C}
\]

Using saturated properties at \(1.2\ \text{MPa}\), \(s_3 \approx 4.007\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\).

Thus:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=(9.1)(4.007)-(4.9)(6.754)-(4.2)(0.477)\approx 1.37\ \text{kW/K}
\]

\[
\dot X_{\text{dest}}=(298.15)(1.37)\approx 407\ \text{kW}
\]

With dead-state properties at \(25^\circ\text{C},\ 0.1\ \text{MPa}\):  
\(h_0 \approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg},\ s_0 \approx 0.367\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\),

the exergy efficiency evaluates to:

\[
\eta_{II}\approx 0.906
\]

h_1 = 2897.0 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 139.3 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 9.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 1624.2 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 188.2 °C  
s_1 = 6.754 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.477 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 4.007 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 1.37 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 407.0 kW  
η_II = 0.906

question:
Two water streams mix adiabatically at 0.7 MPa in a mixing chamber. Stream 1: 130.0°C, 7.8 kg/s. Stream 2: 50.0°C, 9.3 kg/s. The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit temperature, entropy generation rate, exergy destruction rate, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
ṁ_3 = ___ kg/s
h_3 = ___ kJ/kg
T_3 = ___ °C
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_3 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
Ṡ_gen = ___ kW/K
Ẋ_dest = ___ kW
η_II = ___
raw_response:
At \(P=0.7\ \text{MPa}\), the saturation temperature of water is about \(165^\circ\text{C}\), so both inlet streams and the outlet are **compressed liquid**. For compressed liquid water, a standard approximation is

\[
h(T,P)\approx h_f(T), \qquad s(T,P)\approx s_f(T)
\]

using saturated-liquid properties at the same temperature.

### 1) Inlet properties
From liquid-water tables (approx.):

- Stream 1 at \(130^\circ\text{C}\):
  \[
  h_1 \approx 546.6\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 1.633\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

- Stream 2 at \(50^\circ\text{C}\):
  \[
  h_2 \approx 209.3\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_2 \approx 0.7038\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
  \]

### 2) Mass and energy balances
Steady, adiabatic mixing chamber:

\[
\dot m_3=\dot m_1+\dot m_2=7.8+9.3=17.1\ \text{kg/s}
\]

\[
\dot m_1 h_1+\dot m_2 h_2=\dot m_3 h_3
\]

\[
h_3=\frac{(7.8)(546.6)+(9.3)(209.3)}{17.1}\approx 363.2\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

From compressed-liquid water properties, \(h_3\approx 363.2\ \text{kJ/kg}\) corresponds to

\[
T_3 \approx 86.7^\circ\text{C}, \qquad s_3 \approx 1.153\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

### 3) Entropy generation rate
For a steady adiabatic mixer:

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=\dot m_3 s_3-\dot m_1 s_1-\dot m_2 s_2
\]

\[
\dot S_{\text{gen}}=(17.1)(1.153)-(7.8)(1.633)-(9.3)(0.7038)
\approx 0.432\ \text{kW/K}
\]

### 4) Exergy destruction rate
\[
\dot X_{\text{dest}}=T_0\dot S_{\text{gen}}
\]

with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\):

\[
\dot X_{\text{dest}}=(298.15)(0.432)\approx 128.8\ \text{kW}
\]

### 5) Second-law efficiency
Using the usual **overall mixing-chamber exergy efficiency**:

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\dot E_{x,\text{out}}}{\dot E_{x,\text{in}}}
\]

where for each stream

\[
\psi=(h-h_0)-T_0(s-s_0)
\]

At the dead state \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C},\ P_0=0.1\ \text{MPa}\), for liquid water:
\[
h_0\approx 104.9\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_0\approx 0.367\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

Thus,

\[
\psi_1 \approx (546.6-104.9)-298.15(1.633-0.367)\approx 64.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\psi_2 \approx (209.3-104.9)-298.15(0.7038-0.367)\approx 4.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
\psi_3 \approx (363.2-104.9)-298.15(1.153-0.367)\approx 24.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then

\[
\eta_{II}=
\frac{(17.1)(24.1)}{(7.8)(64.4)+(9.3)(4.1)}
\approx 0.763
\]

h_1 = 546.6 kJ/kg  
h_2 = 209.3 kJ/kg  
ṁ_3 = 17.1 kg/s  
h_3 = 363.2 kJ/kg  
T_3 = 86.7 °C  
s_1 = 1.633 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_2 = 0.7038 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_3 = 1.153 kJ/(kg·K)  
Ṡ_gen = 0.432 kW/K  
Ẋ_dest = 128.8 kW  
η_II = 0.763

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 389.0°C, 1.4 MPa with a velocity of 26.0 m/s and exits at 0.7 MPa. The nozzle isentropic efficiency is 91.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle and the nozzle isentropic efficiency:

1. Inlet state from superheated steam tables at \(P_1=1.4\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=389.0^\circ\text{C}\):
\[
h_1 \approx 3228.5\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 7.236\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

2. Isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.7\ \text{MPa}\) with
\[
s_{2s}=s_1
\]
From superheated steam tables at \(0.7\ \text{MPa}\):
\[
h_{2s} \approx 3035.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

3. Nozzle efficiency:
\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.91
\]
So,
\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]
\[
h_2=3228.5-0.91(3228.5-3035.4)=3052.8\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4. Energy equation for the adiabatic nozzle:
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]
with \(V_1=26.0\ \text{m/s}\). Thus,
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)\times 1000}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{26.0^2+2(3228.5-3052.8)\times 1000}=593.4\ \text{m/s}
\]

h_1 = 3228.5 kJ/kg
s_1 = 7.236 kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = 3035.4 kJ/kg
V_2 = 593.4 m/s
h_2 = 3052.8 kJ/kg

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 278.0°C, 4.1 MPa with a velocity of 40.0 m/s and exits at 0.56 MPa. The nozzle isentropic efficiency is 93.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using the adiabatic nozzle energy equation and nozzle isentropic efficiency:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
\]

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
\]

From the superheated steam tables at \(P_1=4.1\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=278.0^\circ\text{C}\):

- \(h_1 \approx 2895.8\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(s_1 \approx 6.2285\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

At the exit pressure \(P_2=0.56\ \text{MPa}\), set \(s_{2s}=s_1\).  
Using saturated properties at \(0.56\ \text{MPa}\), the isentropic exit state is in the wet region, giving:

\[
h_{2s}\approx 2513.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then from nozzle efficiency:

\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n\left(h_1-h_{2s}\right)
\]

\[
h_2=2895.8-0.93(2895.8-2513.6)\approx 2540.4\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now use the steady-flow energy equation:

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(1000)(h_1-h_2)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{40.0^2+2(1000)(2895.8-2540.4)}\approx 844.1\ \text{m/s}
\]

s_1 = 6.2285 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
An adiabatic nozzle receives steam at 430.0°C / 2.9 MPa with V₁ = 79.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.68 MPa and η_nozzle = 98.0%. Calculate the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle efficiency relation,

\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

Steps:
1. From superheated steam tables at \(P_1=2.9\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=430.0^\circ\text{C}\), obtain \(h_1\) and \(s_1\).
2. For the isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.68\ \text{MPa}\), set \(s_{2s}=s_1\) and read/interpolate \(h_{2s}\).
3. Use nozzle efficiency to get the actual exit enthalpy:
   \[
   h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]
4. Then compute the exit velocity:
   \[
   V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2( h_1-h_2 )\times 1000}
   \]

Using interpolated steam-table values gives:

h_1 = 3297.6 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 7.020 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2889.8 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 898.4 m/s  
h_2 = 2898.0 kJ/kg

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
For an adiabatic nozzle,

\[
h_1 + \frac{V_1^2}{2} = h_2 + \frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle efficiency is

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.98
\]

Using superheated steam tables:

- At \(P_1=0.5\text{ MPa},\ T_1=276^\circ\text{C}\), the inlet state is superheated.
- Interpolating in the superheated tables gives approximately  
  \[
  h_1 \approx 3012.0\ \text{kJ/kg}, \qquad s_1 \approx 7.3540\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
  \]

For the isentropic exit state at \(P_2=0.15\text{ MPa}\),

\[
s_{2s}=s_1
\]

Interpolating at \(0.15\text{ MPa}\) for this entropy gives

\[
h_{2s} \approx 2746.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Now apply nozzle efficiency:

\[
h_2 = h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

\[
h_2 = 3012.0 - 0.98(3012.0-2746.0)=2751.3\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Then from the steady-flow energy equation,

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{18.0^2+2000(3012.0-2751.3)}\approx 722.3\ \text{m/s}
\]

At \(P_2=0.15\text{ MPa}\) and \(h_2=2751.3\text{ kJ/kg}\), the exit state is slightly superheated; from the tables,

\[
s_2 \approx 7.3668\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Since the nozzle is adiabatic,

\[
s_{\text{gen}} = s_2-s_1
\]

\[
s_{\text{gen}} = 7.3668-7.3540 = 0.0128\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

h_2s = 2746.0 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 722.3 m/s  
h_2 = 2751.3 kJ/kg

s_gen = 0.0128 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 293.0°C, 3.7 MPa with V₁ = 62.0 m/s and exits at 0.42 MPa. The nozzle efficiency is 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using interpolated steam-table properties:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1=3.7\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=293^\circ\text{C}\) is **superheated vapor**.  
   From the superheated steam tables,
   \[
   h_1 \approx 2947.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_1 \approx 6.3600\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

2. **Isentropic exit state** at \(P_2=0.42\ \text{MPa}\):  
   \[
   s_{2s}=s_1
   \]
   At \(0.42\ \text{MPa}\), the state is in the two-phase region, so using saturated properties,
   \[
   x_{2s}=\frac{s_1-s_f}{s_{fg}},\qquad h_{2s}=h_f+x_{2s}h_{fg}
   \]
   which gives
   \[
   h_{2s}\approx 2522.9\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

3. **Nozzle efficiency**
   \[
   \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.93
   \]
   so
   \[
   h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]
   giving
   \[
   h_2\approx 2552.6\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Exit velocity** from the adiabatic nozzle energy balance:
   \[
   h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
   \]
   with \(V_1=62.0\ \text{m/s}\), yielding
   \[
   V_2\approx 890.3\ \text{m/s}
   \]

5. **Actual exit entropy** at \(P_2=0.42\ \text{MPa}\) and \(h_2\):  
   Since the exit is also in the two-phase region,
   \[
   x_2=\frac{h_2-h_f}{h_{fg}},\qquad s_2=s_f+x_2s_{fg}
   \]
   giving
   \[
   s_2\approx 6.4322\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

6. **Entropy generation per unit mass** for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1
   \]
   so
   \[
   s_{\text{gen}}\approx 0.0722\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

question:
Steam enters an adiabatic nozzle at 431.0°C, 3.3 MPa with V₁ = 74.0 m/s and exits at 0.51 MPa. The nozzle efficiency is 96.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using superheated steam tables and nozzle relations:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1 = 3.3\ \text{MPa},\ T_1 = 431.0^\circ\text{C}\)  
   From superheated steam tables:
   \[
   h_1 \approx 3302.0\ \text{kJ/kg},\qquad s_1 \approx 6.980\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

2. **Isentropic exit state** at \(P_2 = 0.51\ \text{MPa}\)  
   For the isentropic case,
   \[
   s_{2s} = s_1
   \]
   From superheated tables at \(0.51\ \text{MPa}\) and \(s=s_1\):
   \[
   h_{2s} \approx 2825.0\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

3. **Nozzle efficiency**
   \[
   \eta_n = \frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}} = 0.96
   \]
   so
   \[
   h_2 = h_1 - 0.96(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]
   \[
   h_2 = 3302.0 - 0.96(3302.0-2825.0) = 2844.1\ \text{kJ/kg}
   \]

4. **Exit velocity** from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   h_1 + \frac{V_1^2}{2\,1000} = h_2 + \frac{V_2^2}{2\,1000}
   \]
   with \(V_1=74.0\ \text{m/s}\):
   \[
   V_2 = \sqrt{V_1^2 + 2000(h_1-h_2)}
   \]
   \[
   V_2 = \sqrt{74.0^2 + 2000(3302.0-2844.1)} \approx 959.9\ \text{m/s}
   \]

5. **Actual exit entropy**  
   At \(P_2=0.51\ \text{MPa}\) and \(h_2=2844.1\ \text{kJ/kg}\), from superheated tables:
   \[
   s_2 \approx 7.026\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

6. **Entropy generation per unit mass**  
   For an adiabatic nozzle,
   \[
   s_{gen} = s_2 - s_1
   \]
   \[
   s_{gen} = 7.026 - 6.980 = 0.046\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
   \]

question:
Steam at 451.0°C / 4.1 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 70.0 m/s and exits at 0.62 MPa (η_nozzle = 92.0%). The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using superheated-steam properties and the adiabatic nozzle relations:

\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

\[
\frac{V_2^2-V_1^2}{2}=1000\,(h_1-h_2)
\]

For an adiabatic single-inlet/single-exit nozzle,

\[
s_{\text{gen}} = s_2-s_1
\]

\[
x_{\text{dest}} = T_0\,s_{\text{gen}}
\]

and the second-law efficiency is

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke + x_{\text{dest}}}
=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{\text{dest}}}
\]

Steam-table interpolation gives the inlet state at \(4.1\ \text{MPa},\ 451^\circ\text{C}\), then the isentropic exit state at \(0.62\ \text{MPa}\) with \(s_{2s}=s_1\), and finally the actual exit state from nozzle efficiency.

Final answers:

h_1 = 3342.0 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 6.931 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 2845.2 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 958 m/s  
h_2 = 2884.9 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 7.020 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.089 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 26.5 kJ/kg

question:
Steam at 315.0°C / 1.6 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 12.0 m/s and exits at 0.38 MPa (η_nozzle = 97.0%). The dead state is T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Determine the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg

h_2s = ___ kJ/kg

h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using superheated steam tables and standard nozzle/exergy relations:

1. **Inlet state** at \(P_1=1.6\ \text{MPa},\ T_1=315.0^\circ\text{C}\):
   \[
   h_1,\ s_1 \quad \text{from superheated steam tables}
   \]

2. **Isentropic exit state** at \(P_2=0.38\ \text{MPa}\):
   \[
   s_{2s}=s_1
   \]
   From the tables at \(0.38\ \text{MPa}\), this gives \(h_{2s}\).

3. **Nozzle efficiency**:
   \[
   \eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
   \]
   so
   \[
   h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
   \]

4. **Exit velocity** from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
   \[
   h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
   \]
   \[
   V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2\times 1000\,(h_1-h_2)}
   \]

5. **Actual exit entropy** \(s_2\) is obtained from the steam tables at \(P_2=0.38\ \text{MPa}\) and \(h_2\).

6. **Entropy generation** for an adiabatic one-in/one-out nozzle:
   \[
   s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1
   \]

7. **Exergy destruction**:
   \[
   x_{\text{dest}}=T_0\,s_{\text{gen}}
   \]
   with \(T_0=25^\circ\text{C}=298.15\ \text{K}\).

8. **Second-law efficiency** for a nozzle:
   \[
   \eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta \psi_{\text{flow}}}
   =\frac{(V_2^2-V_1^2)/2}{(h_1-h_2)-T_0(s_1-s_2)}
   =\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{\text{dest}}}
   \]

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 622.0 K and 0.51 MPa with a velocity of 50.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.18 MPa and η_nozzle = 95.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
For a steady, adiabatic nozzle with negligible potential-energy change,

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

and the nozzle efficiency is

\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]

For ideal-gas air with constant \(c_p\),

\[
h=c_pT
\]

\]

\[

\]

### 2) Inlet entropy

\]

### 3) Isentropic exit state

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 561.0 K and 0.49 MPa with a velocity of 98.0 m/s. The exit pressure is 0.15 MPa and η_nozzle = 94.0%. Determine the exit velocity.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
raw_response:
Using constant specific heats for air:

- \(h = c_p T\)
- For the isentropic exit state:
  \[
  \frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}=\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
  \]
- Nozzle efficiency:
  \[
  \eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
  \]
- Adiabatic nozzle energy balance:
  \[
  h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
  \]
  with \(h\) in kJ/kg and \(V\) in m/s.

Calculations:

\[
h_1=(1.005)(561.0)=563.805\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Since no entropy reference state is specified, take state 1 as the entropy datum, so \(s_1=0\) and \(s_{2s}=s_1\).

\[
T_{2s}=561.0\left(\frac{0.15}{0.49}\right)^{(1.4-1)/1.4}=399.97\ \text{K}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=(1.005)(399.97)=401.97\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
0.94=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1-0.94(h_1-h_{2s})=411.68\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}
=\sqrt{98.0^2+2000(563.805-411.68)}
=560.24\ \text{m/s}
\]

h_1 = 563.81 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.000 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 401.97 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 560.24 m/s  
h_2 = 411.68 kJ/kg

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) enters an adiabatic nozzle at 846.0 K / 0.48 MPa with V₁ = 59.0 m/s. It exits at 0.2 MPa with η_nozzle = 93.0%. Find the exit velocity and entropy generation.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using constant-\( c_p \) ideal-gas air:

\[
h_1=c_pT_1
\]

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
\]

\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}
\]

For a nozzle,
\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
h_2=h_1-\eta_{\text{nozzle}}(h_1-h_{2s})
\]

Then from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle,
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2000}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2000}
\]
(with \(V^2/2000\) in kJ/kg).

Finally,
\[
\Delta s=s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\]
and since the nozzle is adiabatic,
\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1
\]

Numerical results:

- \(h_1=1.005(846.0)=850.23\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(T_{2s}=846.0(0.2/0.48)^{0.4/1.4}=658.78\ \text{K}\)
- \(h_{2s}=1.005(658.78)=662.07\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(h_2=850.23-0.93(850.23-662.07)=675.24\ \text{kJ/kg}\)
- \(T_2=h_2/c_p=671.88\ \text{K}\)
- \(V_2=\sqrt{59.0^2+2000(850.23-675.24)}=594.52\ \text{m/s}\)
- \(\Delta s = 1.005\ln(671.88/846.0)-0.287\ln(0.2/0.48)=0.0197\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}\)

Since absolute entropy needs a reference, take state 1 as the entropy datum: \(s_1=0\).

h_1 = 850.23 kJ/kg
s_1 = 0.0000 kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = 662.07 kJ/kg
V_2 = 594.52 m/s
h_2 = 675.24 kJ/kg
s_2 = 0.0197 kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = 0.0197 kJ/(kg·K)

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg

h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
raw_response:
Using the constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas model for air:

1. Inlet enthalpy
\[
h_1=c_pT_1=(1.005)(622.0)=625.11\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2. Isentropic exit state:
\[
\frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}=\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=\left(\frac{0.12}{0.81}\right)^{0.4/1.4}
\]
\[
T_{2s}=360.45\ \text{K}
\]
\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=(1.005)(360.45)=362.25\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

3. Nozzle efficiency
\[
\eta_n=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}
\]
\[
h_2=h_1-\eta_n(h_1-h_{2s})
=625.11-0.93(625.11-362.25)=380.65\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

4. Exit velocity from the steady-flow energy equation for an adiabatic nozzle:
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2(h_1-h_2)(1000)}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{59.0^2+2(244.46)(1000)}=701.71\ \text{m/s}
\]

5. Entropy generation per unit mass:
\[
s_2-s_1=c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\]
with
\[
T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}=\frac{380.65}{1.005}=378.76\ \text{K}
\]
\[
s_{gen}=s_2-s_1=0.0495\ \text{kJ/(kg\cdot K)}
\]

For the individual entropy values below, I use the common ideal-gas reference \(s=c_p\ln T-R\ln P\) with \(T\) in K and \(P\) in kPa.

s_1 = 4.5431 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 362.25 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 701.71 m/s  
h_2 = 380.65 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 4.5926 kJ/(kg·K)

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 621.0 K / 0.83 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 93.0 m/s and exits at 0.28 MPa (η_nozzle = 94.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using \(h=c_pT\) for ideal-gas air, and entropy relative to the dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):

1. **Isentropic exit state**
\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{(k-1)/k}
=621\left(\frac{0.28}{0.83}\right)^{0.4/1.4}
\approx 455.33\ \text{K}
\]
\[
h_1=c_pT_1=1.005(621)=624.11\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]
\[
h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=1.005(455.33)=457.61\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

2. **Actual exit enthalpy from nozzle efficiency**
\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.94
\]
\[
h_2=h_1-0.94(h_1-h_{2s})
=467.60\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

3. **Exit velocity from steady-flow energy equation**
(adabatic nozzle, negligible potential energy change)
\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2}=h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]
\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2\times 1000\,(h_1-h_2)}
=\sqrt{93^2+2(1000)(156.51)}
\approx 567.15\ \text{m/s}
\]

4. **Entropy at states 1 and 2**
\[
s-cst = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P}{P_0}\right)
\]
\[
s_1=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{621}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.83}{0.1}\right)
\approx 0.1300\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]
\[
s_2=1.005\ln\!\left(\frac{465.27}{298.15}\right)-0.287\ln\!\left(\frac{0.28}{0.1}\right)
\approx 0.1517\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

For an adiabatic nozzle:
\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1\approx 0.0217\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

5. **Exergy destruction**
\[
x_{\text{dest}}=T_0\,s_{\text{gen}}
=298.15(0.0217)\approx 6.47\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

6. **Second-law efficiency**
For a nozzle, the desired product is the kinetic-energy increase:
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke+x_{\text{dest}}}
=\frac{h_1-h_2}{(h_1-h_2)+x_{\text{dest}}}
\]
\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{156.51}{156.51+6.47}\approx 0.9603
\]

h_1 = 624.11 kJ/kg  
s_1 = 0.1300 kJ/(kg·K)  
h_2s = 457.61 kJ/kg  
V_2 = 567.15 m/s  
h_2 = 467.60 kJ/kg  
s_2 = 0.1517 kJ/(kg·K)  
s_gen = 0.0217 kJ/(kg·K)  
x_dest = 6.47 kJ/kg  
η_II = 0.9603

question:
Air (ideal gas, c_p = 1.005 kJ/(kg·K), k = 1.4, R = 0.287 kJ/(kg·K)) at 675.0 K / 0.52 MPa enters an adiabatic nozzle at 53.0 m/s and exits at 0.15 MPa (η_nozzle = 97.0%). T₀ = 298.15 K, P₀ = 0.1 MPa. Calculate the exit velocity, entropy generation, exergy destruction, and second-law efficiency.

Present your final answers in the following format:
h_1 = ___ kJ/kg
s_1 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = ___ kJ/kg
V_2 = ___ m/s
h_2 = ___ kJ/kg
s_2 = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = ___ kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = ___ kJ/kg
η_II = ___
raw_response:
Using constant-\(c_p\) ideal-gas air and taking entropy relative to the dead state \((T_0,P_0)\):

\[
h = c_p T,\qquad s-s_0 = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P}{P_0}\right)
\]

For the isentropic exit state, I used the relation consistent with the given \(c_p\) and \(R\):

\[
0=\Delta s = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_{2s}}{T_1}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)
\]

so

\[
T_{2s}=T_1\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right)^{R/c_p}=473.28\ \text{K}
\]

Then

\[
h_1=c_pT_1=678.38,\qquad h_{2s}=c_pT_{2s}=475.65\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Nozzle efficiency:

\[
\eta_{\text{nozzle}}=\frac{h_1-h_2}{h_1-h_{2s}}=0.97
\]

gives

\[
h_2 = h_1 - 0.97(h_1-h_{2s}) = 481.73\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Adiabatic nozzle energy equation:

\[
h_1+\frac{V_1^2}{2} = h_2+\frac{V_2^2}{2}
\]

with \(1\ \text{kJ/kg}=1000\ \text{m}^2/\text{s}^2\), gives

\[
V_2=\sqrt{V_1^2+2000(h_1-h_2)}=629.36\ \text{m/s}
\]

Exit temperature:

\[
T_2=\frac{h_2}{c_p}=479.33\ \text{K}
\]

Entropy at states 1 and 2:

\[
s_1 = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_1}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_1}{P_0}\right)=0.3480
\]

\[
s_2 = c_p\ln\!\left(\frac{T_2}{T_0}\right)-R\ln\!\left(\frac{P_2}{P_0}\right)=0.3608
\]

Since the nozzle is adiabatic,

\[
s_{\text{gen}}=s_2-s_1=0.01277\ \text{kJ/(kg·K)}
\]

Exergy destruction:

\[
x_{\text{dest}} = T_0 s_{\text{gen}} = 298.15(0.01277)=3.81\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

Using the exergy-efficiency definition for a nozzle,

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{\Delta ke}{\Delta ke + x_{\text{dest}}}
\]

with

\[
\Delta ke=\frac{V_2^2-V_1^2}{2}=h_1-h_2=196.64\ \text{kJ/kg}
\]

so

\[
\eta_{II}=\frac{196.64}{196.64+3.81}=0.9810
\]

h_1 = 678.38 kJ/kg
s_1 = 0.3480 kJ/(kg·K)
h_2s = 475.65 kJ/kg
V_2 = 629.36 m/s
h_2 = 481.73 kJ/kg
s_2 = 0.3608 kJ/(kg·K)
s_gen = 0.01277 kJ/(kg·K)
x_dest = 3.81 kJ/kg
η_II = 0.9810
